ops.title: cc-sudo Phase 5 mechanics spike: SE/audit-token/prompt findings
ops.body:
Spike harness: /Users/yasyf/Code/wt-cc-sudo/spike (se_probe/audit_probe/la_probe + build.sh + RUNBOOK.md). Observed on macOS 26.5, arm64, Team SXKCTF23Q2.

Q3 (SE keygen signing requirement) — ANSWERED:
- Unsigned (sig stripped) binary → SIGKILL at exec (exit 137, AMFI).
- Ephemeral (non-permanent) SE P-256 keygen + ECDSA-SHA256 sign → WORKS ad-hoc-signed (exit 0). Full SE-sign → SecKeyVerifySignature roundtrip verifies good sig, rejects tampered, ad-hoc. SE crypto needs only *a* code signature, not Developer-ID.
- Permanent (kSecAttrIsPermanent=true, keychain-persisted) SE key → -34018 errSecMissingEntitlement on BOTH ad-hoc AND Developer-ID-without-provisioning-profile. Gate = keychain-access-group entitlement, needs Dev-ID sig + embedded provisioning profile.
- Confound: screen locked → WhenUnlocked keys fail earlier with -25308 (keybag). Isolated signing via AfterFirstUnlock ACL to surface the true -34018.
- Consequence: authkit enrolled/persistent SE key requires signed+provisioned bundle; dev builds get ephemeral only. SEAttestor + cc-sudo Verifier crypto are unit-testable ad-hoc with ephemeral keys.

Q2 (audit-token caller validation) — ANSWERED (user-level API mechanics; root-context is interactive):
- audit-token → SecCodeCopyGuestWithAttributes(kSecGuestAttributeAudit) → SecCodeCheckValidity works between two live user processes. Team-ID DR: PASS (0) correct team, REJECT wrong-team + ad-hoc caller with -67050. Matches plan's "wrong Team ID rejected" negative.
- Open nuance: exec/stdin helper model makes the invoker's audit token self-reported → spoofable (token not secret). Robust = XPC peer token or lean on reverse bundle-path DR pin (SecStaticCodeCreateWithPath). Decide before Phase 3 freezes consent-sign stdin contract.

Q1 (root→asuser→Touch ID sheet) — NEEDS INTERACTIVE RUN (sudo + tap):
- canEvaluatePolicy=true even locked + setsid/nohup-detached (capability check only, not proof sheet renders). biometryType raw=1, Touch ID confirmed via bioutil.
- launchctl: gui/501 = type=login session=Aqua creator=loginwindow euid=0 handle 100023; user/501 = session=Background gui asid=100023. `launchctl asuser 501` targets the user domain bound to the console GUI audit session.
- Interactive steps (RUNBOOK Steps 2-3) decide: Terminal-descended vs SSH-descended asuser prompt. Step 3 (SSH) FAIL ⇒ synckitd Aqua agent MANDATORY for local non-GUI prompts; PASS ⇒ cc-sudo-exec asuser suffices, synckitd only for locked-Mac scenario (b).
